title: जगन्नाथाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
कदाचित्कालिन्दीतटविपिनसङ्गीतकवरो
मुदा गोपीनारीवदनकमलास्वादमधुपः।
रमाशम्भुब्रह्मामरपतिगणेशार्चितपदो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥१॥

भुजे सव्ये वेणुं शिरसि शिखिपिञ्छं कटितटे
दुकूलं नेत्रान्ते सहचरकटाक्षं विदधते।
सदा श्रीमद्वृन्दावनवसतिलीलापरिचयो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥२॥

महाम्भोधेस्तीरे कनकरुचिरे नीलशिखरे
वसन्प्रासादान्तः सहजबलभद्रेण बलिना।
सुभद्रामध्यस्थः सकलसुरसेवावसरदो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥३॥

कृपापारावारः सजलजलदश्रेणिरुचिरो
रमावाणीरामः स्फुरदमलपङ्केरुहमुखः।
सुरेन्द्रैराराध्यः श्रुतिगणशिखागीतचरितो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥४॥

रथारूढो गच्छन्पथि मिलितभूदेवपटलैः
स्तुतिप्रादुर्भावं प्रतिपदमुपाकर्ण्य सदयः।
दयासिन्धुर्बन्धुः सकलजगतां सिन्धुसुतया
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥५॥

परब्रह्मापीडः कुवलयदलोत्फुल्लनयनो
निवासी नीलाद्रौ जगदवनकारी करुणया।
न दुर्गादेवी वा न च कमला वा न च शची
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥६॥

न वै याचे राज्यं न च कनकमाणिक्यविभवं
न याचेऽहं रम्यां सकलजनकाम्यां वरवधूम्।
सदा काले काले प्रमथपतिना गीतचरितो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥७॥

हर त्वं संसारं द्रुततरमसारं सुरपते
हर त्वं पापानां विततिमपरां यादवपते।
अहो दीनानाथं निहितमचलं निश्चितपदं
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयनपथगामी भवतु मे॥८॥

जगन्नाथाष्टकं पुण्यं यः पठेत्प्रयतः शुचिः।
सर्वपापविशुद्धात्मा विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥
transliteration:
Kadachit-Kalindi-Tata-Vipina-Sangita-Kavaro
Muda Gopi-Nari-Vadana-Kamala-Asvada-Madhupah.
Rama-Shambhu-Brahma-Amara-Pati-Ganesha-Archita-Pado
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||1||

Bhuje Savye Venum Shirasi Shikhi-Pinchham Kati-Tate
Dukulam Netra-Ante Sahachara-Kataksham Vidadhate.
Sada Shrimad-Vrindavana-Vasati-Lila-Parichayo
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||2||

Mahambhodhes-Tire Kanaka-Ruchire Nila-Shikhare
Vasan-Prasadantah Sahaja-Balabhadrena Balina.
Subhadra-Madhyasthah Sakala-Sura-Seva-Avasarado
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||3||

Kripa-Para-Avarah Sajala-Jalada-Shreni-Ruchiro
Rama-Vani-Ramah Sphurad-Amala-Pankeruha-Mukhah.
Surendrair-Aradhyah Shruti-Gana-Shikha-Gita-Charito
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||4||

Ratha-Arudho Gachchhan-Pathi Milita-Bhudeva-Patalaih
Stuti-Pradurbhavam Pratipada-Mupakarnya Sadayah.
Daya-Sindhur-Bandhuh Sakala-Jagatam Sindhu-Sutaya
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||5||

Parabrahma-Apidah Kuvalaya-Dala-Utphulla-Nayano
Nivasi Niladrau Jagad-Avana-Kari Karunaya.
Na Durga-Devi Va Na Cha Kamala Va Na Cha Shachi
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||6||

Na Vai Yache Rajyam Na Cha Kanaka-Manikya-Vibhavam
Na Yache-Aham Ramyam Sakala-Jana-Kamyam Vara-Vadhum.
Sada Kale Kale Pramatha-Patina Gita-Charito
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||7||

Hara Tvam Samsaram Drutataram-Asaram Sura-Pate
Hara Tvam Papanam Vitatim-Aparam Yadava-Pate.
Aho Dina-Anatham Nihitam-Achalam Nishchita-Padam
Jagannathah Svami Nayana-Patha-Gami Bhavatu Me.||8||

Jagannatha-Ashtakam Punyam Yah Pathet-Prayatah Shuchih.
Sarva-Papa-Vishuddha-Atma Vishnu-Lokam Sa Gachchhati.
hindiMeaning:
जो कभी कालिन्दी (यमुना) के तट पर वन में मधुर संगीत गाते थे, जो प्रसन्नतापूर्वक गोपी-नारियों के कमल-मुख का रस-आस्वादन करने वाले भ्रमर हैं, जिनके चरणों की रमा, शम्भु, ब्रह्मा, इन्द्र और गणेश पूजा करते हैं - वे जगन्नाथ स्वामी मेरे नयनों के मार्ग में आएँ (अर्थात मुझे उनके दर्शन हों)।

जिनके बाएँ हाथ में वेणु (बाँसुरी) है, सिर पर मोरपंख है, कटि पर दुकूल (रेशमी वस्त्र) है, नेत्रों के कोनों से सहचरी (राधा) पर कटाक्ष करते हैं, जो सदैव श्रीमद् वृन्दावन में विहार-लीला करते हैं - वे जगन्नाथ स्वामी मेरे नयनों के मार्ग में आएँ।

मैं न तो राज्य माँगता हूँ, न सोने-मणियों की सम्पत्ति, न ही सुन्दर वधू। बस, सदा-सदा प्रमथपति (शिव) द्वारा गाए गए चरित वाले जगन्नाथ स्वामी मेरे नयनों के मार्ग में आएँ।
benefits: Invokes the divine darshan of lord jagannath
Purifies the soul and destroys all sins
Leads the devotee to Vishnu-loka after death
Invokes the blessing of detachment from worldly desires
Especially powerful during Rath Yatra festival
Develops deep devotion and surrender to the Lord
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Jagannath, Jagannatha Ashtakam (जगन्नाथाष्टकम्) stands as an eight-verse devotional hymn. Attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow detachment from worldly desires and purify the soul and destroy all sins. It is also said to lead the devotee to Vishnu-loka after death. This prayer is traditionally recited during Janmashtami to invoke divine grace. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Jagannatha Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Jagannatha Ashtakam by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses seeking the divine vision of Lord Jagannath.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Jagannatha Ashtakam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Jagannatha Ashtakam (जगन्नाथाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya in praise of Lord Jagannath of Puri — the unique form of Krishna/Vishnu worshipped along with brother Balabhadra and sister Subhadra at the famous 12th-century temple in Puri, Odisha. The Ashtakam is among Shankara's most popular Vaishnava stotras and opens with the celebrated verse Kadachit Kalindi Tata Vipina Sangita Kavaro — He who once excelled in singing in the forest groves on the bank of the Kalindi (Yamuna), the bee who tasted the lotus-faces of the gopis, whose feet are worshipped by Rama, Shambhu, Brahma, Indra and Ganesha — may that Lord Jagannath come into the path of my eyes. The hymn ends with a phalashruti promising attainment of Vishnu-loka to one who recites it with devotion. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya stotra-ratnavali; classical Vaishnava tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between jagannath-ashtakam and jagannatha-ashtakam slugs?
seoFaqs.answer: Both jagannath-ashtakam and jagannatha-ashtakam refer to the same Sanskrit composition by Adi Shankaracharya — they are simply transliteration variants of the same eight-verse hymn beginning with Kadachit Kalindi Tata. The variation reflects two common Sanskrit-to-English conventions: (1) Jagannath without trailing 'a' follows the Hindi-influenced Anglicized convention popular in news media, English signage and modern colloquial usage; (2) Jagannatha with trailing 'a' follows the strict Sanskrit IAST transliteration where the inherent vowel 'a' at the end of the noun (jagat-natha — Lord of the world) is preserved. Both are valid; both refer to the same Puri Jagannath Ashtakam. The text in both pages is the same Shankara composition. The duplicate exists for SEO accessibility — devotees searching either spelling will find the hymn. (Sources: Sanskrit transliteration conventions; IAST standard.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Kadachit Kalindi Tata Vipina Sangita Kavaro?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated opening verse of the Jagannatha Ashtakam describes Lord Jagannath in his Krishna form: He (Jagannath) who once excelled (kavaro) in singing (sangita) in the forest groves (vipina) on the bank (tata) of the Kalindi (Yamuna river), the joyful bee (madhupah) who tasted (asvada) the lotus-faces (vadana-kamala) of the gopi-women (gopi-nari), whose feet (pado) are worshipped (archita) by Rama (Lakshmi), Shambhu (Shiva), Brahma, Amara-pati (Indra) and Ganesha — may that Jagannath, the Lord (Swami), become a goer on the path of my eyes (Nayana Patha-gami Bhavatu Me). The verse establishes that Jagannath of Puri is none other than the divine cowherd Krishna of Vrindavan, beloved of the gopis, the supreme Vaishnava deity worshipped by all the major gods. (Sources: Jagannatha Ashtakam verse 1; Adi Shankara Vaishnava stotras.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the phalashruti of Jagannatha Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Jagannatha Ashtakam concludes with a powerful phalashruti (declaration of fruits) verse: Jagannatha-Ashtakam Punyam Yah Pathet-Prayatah Shuchih, Sarva-Papa-Vishuddha-Atma Vishnu-Lokam Sa Gachchhati — One who recites this auspicious Jagannath Ashtakam with devotion and ritual purity becomes purified of all sins and attains Vishnu-loka (Vaikuntha). The phalashruti makes the Ashtakam not merely a poetic expression but a moksha-prada (liberation-bestowing) stotra. The promise of Vishnu-loka attainment after death is one of the supreme rewards in Vaishnava theology — equivalent to the fruit of pilgrimage to Puri Jagannath itself, of Rath Yatra darshan, of Mahaprasad consumption, and of dying within the precincts of Puri (where Lord Jagannath is believed to personally grant moksha). The phalashruti is the basis for the daily recitation of this Ashtakam by lakhs of Vaishnava devotees. (Sources: Jagannatha Ashtakam phalashruti; Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Jagannath worshipped along with Balabhadra and Subhadra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Puri Jagannath sanctum uniquely enshrines three deities together — Lord Jagannath (Krishna, blue-black), Lord Balabhadra (Krishna's elder brother Balarama, white), and Goddess Subhadra (their sister, yellow) — collectively called the Puri Triad. The third verse of the Jagannatha Ashtakam explicitly invokes this triad: Mahambhodhes-Tire Kanaka-Ruchire Nila-Shikhare Vasan-Prasadantah Sahaja-Balabhadrena Balina, Subhadra-Madhyasthah — On the shore of the great ocean (Bay of Bengal), in a golden palace on the Nila hill, dwelling with his powerful brother Balabhadra, with sister Subhadra in the middle. Theologically the triad represents: Jagannath as Para-Brahman / Krishna, Balabhadra as Sankarshana / Adishesha (cosmic foundation), and Subhadra as Yogamaya / Adi-Shakti. Together they are also identified with the trinity of jiva (soul), Ishvara (Lord), and Maya (illusion). The triad iconography is unique to Puri among all Vishnu temples. (Sources: Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda; Puri Mandir tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Jagannatha Ashtakam most auspicious to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Jagannatha Ashtakam is most auspiciously recited on: Krishna Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — September); Puri Rath Yatra (Ashadha Shukla Dwitiya — June/July); Snana Yatra (Jyeshtha Purnima — May/June, the ceremonial bathing of Jagannath); Anavasara period (the 15 days when Jagannath is in seclusion before Rath Yatra); Nilachakra Pradakshina (circumambulation of Puri); Bahuda Yatra (return journey after Rath Yatra); every Ekadashi (sacred to Vishnu); every Thursday (Guruvar); on personal moksha-sankalpa days; at the time of antima samskara (final rites); and daily during morning sandhya by Vaishnavas. Pilgrims to Puri recite the Ashtakam on the temple steps, before climbing the 22 steps (Baisi Pahacha) to the sanctum, and during their entire stay at the dham. ISKCON and Gaudiya Vaishnava traditions chant the Ashtakam during Rath Yatra processions worldwide. (Sources: Puri Jagannath paddhati; Vaishnava parayanam tradition.)
